contextBlurb: Maine has 8 relevant bills tracked in 2025–2026 — 1 on data-center policy and 7 on AI regulation. 3 have been enacted into law. 5 impose active bans or moratoriums.

legislation.title: An Act to Limit the Amount of Electricity That May Be Provided to Data Centers on a Certain Commercial or Industrial Site
legislation.summary: This bill caps the amount of electricity that can be supplied to data centers on commercial or industrial sites in Maine. It is designed to limit the grid load and energy consumption associated with large data center operations.

legislation.title: An Act to Regulate and Prevent Children's Access to Artificial Intelligence Chatbots with Human-like Features and Social Artificial Intelligence Companions
legislation.summary: This Maine bill would regulate or ban children's access to AI chatbots that mimic human conversation and AI social companions. It aims to protect minors from potentially harmful interactions with human-like AI systems.

legislation.title: An Act to Establish Additional Requirements for Dentists to Administer Botulinum Toxin and Dermal Fillers
legislation.summary: This bill sets requirements for dentists who administer Botox and dermal fillers. It has no connection to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: An Act to Update Juvenile Justice System Reporting Requirements and to Direct the Department of Corrections and the Department of Health and Human Services to Study Modifying the Long Creek Youth Development Center
legislation.summary: This bill updates reporting requirements for Maine's juvenile justice system and directs state agencies to study changes to the Long Creek Youth Development Center. It has no connection to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Resolve, Requiring the Maine Health Data Organization to Develop a Plan for Measuring Gaps in Home and Community-based Services
legislation.summary: This bill directs a Maine health data organization to create a plan for measuring gaps in home and community-based services. It is not related to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: An Act to Ensure Human Oversight in Medical Insurance Payment Decisions
legislation.summary: This bill requires that humans, not automated systems, make final decisions about medical insurance coverage and payments. It ensures that AI or algorithmic tools cannot independently deny or approve claims without human review.

legislation.title: An Act to Ensure Transparency in Consumer Transactions Involving Artificial Intelligence
legislation.summary: This bill requires businesses to be upfront with consumers when they are interacting with or being affected by artificial intelligence in commercial transactions. It aims to ensure people know when AI is being used in decisions or communications that affect them as customers.

legislation.title: An Act to Ensure Determinations Made by the State Are Free from Unethical, Unsafe or Illegal Interference by Artificial Intelligence
legislation.summary: This Maine bill aims to prevent artificial intelligence from improperly influencing or making official state government decisions. It sets rules to ensure that AI does not interfere with government determinations in ways that are unethical, unsafe, or illegal.

legislation.title: An Act to Protect Minors from Harmful Depictions Generated by Artificial Intelligence
legislation.summary: This bill aims to protect children by prohibiting AI-generated harmful depictions of minors. It establishes legal safeguards against the creation or distribution of such content.

legislation.title: An Act to Prohibit the Use of Artificial Intelligence in the Denial of Health Insurance Claims
legislation.summary: This Maine bill would ban health insurance companies from using artificial intelligence to deny claims, requiring human decision-making in coverage denials. It aims to protect patients from automated rejections of their health insurance claims.

legislation.title: An Act to Amend the Laws Relating to the State's Background Check Center
legislation.summary: This bill makes amendments to Maine's Background Check Center, which handles criminal history and background screening. It does not appear to relate to AI regulation or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Resolve, Directing the Maine Arts Commission to Study Federal and National Efforts to Protect Artists from Copyright Infringement by Artificial Intelligence Companies and Users and to Monitor Educational Use
legislation.summary: This bill directs Maine's Arts Commission to study how federal and other states' laws are protecting artists from having their work used without permission by AI companies. The commission would also monitor how AI is being used in educational settings.
